featuredPost.title: The Silent Architecture of the Modern Web: How Edge Networks Changed Everything
featuredPost.excerpt: We barely notice the milliseconds it takes to load a webpage, yet beneath the surface lies a terrifyingly sprawling global infrastructure of edge nodes, caching layers, and fiber-optic cables that dictate our digital reality.
featuredPost.author: Sarah Jenkins

secondaryPosts.title: The Resurgence of Brutalist Architecture in Digital Spaces

latestPosts.title: Global Trade Routes in a Decoupling World
